notes.type_correctness: isSmashCapable accepts string | null | undefined matching the optional provider from useDefaultSpecModel. smashCapable prop has correct optional boolean with default=true maintaining backward compat.
notes.pattern_adherence: Pure utility in lib/, prop threading via component props, server-side defence-in-depth — all match existing patterns in the codebase.
notes.test_coverage: All five branches of isSmashCapable tested. ContextScopeSlider tests cover smashCapable=false with contractRefine=true, and the backward-compat default. ProposeSpecModal tests cover codex provider hiding at Max and Hub presets. Server test covers codex stripping and claude preservation.
notes.security: Server strips contractRefine:true from non-claude providers unconditionally, preventing any client-side bypass from triggering Contract Layer spawns against Codex projects.
notes.architectural_alignment: Capability guard lives in lib/ (pure, no React), threaded via props to ContextScopeSlider, wired at ProposeSpecModal (the single decision boundary). Server defence-in-depth at POST /chat/conversations matches the existing normalizeContextScope pattern.
